hero.title: The only app that listens to your engine
hero.subtitle: Intelligent car diagnostics powered by artificial intelligence. Scan the sounds, read the codes, understand your car.
hero.badgeText: AI engine audio scan available in beta

features.title: 12 innovations changing diagnostics
features.subtitle: AutoDiag EU combines artificial intelligence, sound analysis and your phone's sensors for a complete diagnostic.
features.audioScanDesc: Our AI listens to your engine and detects 11 types of faults by sound, with 91.3% accuracy.
features.cabinScanDesc: 7 classes of interior noises detected to identify comfort and safety issues.
features.visionAIDesc: Instant recognition of codes and parts from a photo. Point, snap, understand.
features.aiMechanicDesc: Ask your questions in plain English. Our AI answers based on your specific vehicle.
features.driveTestDesc: Accelerometer, gyroscope, GPS and your phone's microphone for diagnostics on the move.
features.rpmFFTDesc: Engine speed measurement through sound frequency analysis, without an OBD2 connection.
features.shockAbsorberDesc: Damping ratio analysis via bounce test using your phone's built-in sensors.
features.virtualDynoDesc: Real-time engine power estimation via resistive forces and acceleration data.
features.clutchSlipDesc: Slip detection by correlating audio RPM with GPS speed readings.
features.brakeDiscDesc: Frequency analysis of vibrations during braking to detect disc warping.
features.acousticPrintDesc: Your vehicle's unique sound signature to track its condition over time.
features.fleetScoreDesc: Weibull model to predict breakdowns and optimise fleet maintenance.

pricing.title: Transparent pricing

pricing.metaTitle: AutoDiag EU pricing — Free or Premium
pricing.metaDescription: Free plan or Premium at £42/year. 7-day free trial. Complete car diagnostics with AI audio scan.
pricing.pageSubtitle: Start for free. Upgrade to Premium when you need it.
pricing.freeDesc: Everything you need to discover AutoDiag EU
pricing.vsSubtitle: See how AutoDiag EU compares with other OBD2 apps.
pricing.readyDesc: Try Premium free for 7 days, no credit card required.
pricing.faq_freeReally_q: Is the free plan really free?
pricing.faq_freeReally_a: Yes, 100%. Engine audio scan, fault code reading and basic live data are included at no cost. No adverts, no data resale.
pricing.faq_premiumMore_a: Unlimited scans, fault code clearing, repair quotes, pre-MOT check for 6 countries, PDF export, Drive Test without dongle and all OBD2 PIDs.
pricing.faq_trialHow_q: How does the free trial work?
pricing.faq_trialHow_a: Activate Premium and enjoy every feature for 7 days. No credit card required. If you don’t upgrade, you revert to the free plan.
pricing.faq_payment_a: Exclusively via Google Play Billing. Annual automatic renewal, cancellable at any time from your Google Play account.
pricing.faq_refund_a: Yes. Automatic refund within 48 hours via Google Play. After that, contact us at info@autodiag-eu.com and we’ll process your request.

compatibility.title: Is your vehicle compatible?
compatibility.subtitle: Check your car's compatibility with AutoDiag EU across 677 tested vehicles.
compatibility.metaDescription: Check whether your car is compatible with AutoDiag EU. 677 European vehicles tested with OBD2 protocols.
compatibility.pageSubtitle: Check your car’s compatibility with AutoDiag EU across 677 tested vehicles.
compatibility.protocolDesc: OBD2 communication protocol used by your vehicle.
compatibility.pidsDesc: Number of parameters available in real time.
compatibility.yearsCoveredDesc: Model years covered by the database.
compatibility.compatibleCTA: Your {brand} {model} is compatible! Scan it now.
compatibility.howParagraph1: AutoDiag EU uses the OBD2 port fitted to every car sold in Europe since 2001 (petrol) and 2004 (diesel).
compatibility.howParagraph2: Our app communicates with the engine control unit via a Bluetooth ELM327 adapter, supporting CAN, KWP2000, ISO 9141 and J1850 protocols.
compatibility.howParagraph3: All 677 vehicles in our database have been individually tested to guarantee real — not theoretical — compatibility.
compatibility.howParagraph4: Your car isn’t on the list? Get in touch and we’ll test it for you.

about.title: The AutoDiag EU story
about.subtitle: Born from a passion for cars and a frustration with existing tools.
about.metaDescription: Discover the story of AutoDiag EU, born from Reda Kaouani’s passion for cars and the need for a reliable, affordable diagnostic tool.
about.locationSubtitle: Boncourt, Switzerland — Founded by Reda Kaouani
about.quote1: I’ve been passionate about cars since I was a kid — I’ve always loved everything about the motor trade. I’ve done all sorts of jobs in the automotive world. Tools have evolved over time; mechanics isn’t done the way it was 20 years ago. Yet the tools for a clear diagnostic aren’t always affordable and aren’t always kept up to date.
about.quote2: I decided to create a tool that’s simple to use, reliable and always up to date. AutoDiag EU is a community of enthusiasts and casual drivers, expert mechanics and complete novices who simply want to know how healthy their vehicle is. Professionals are on board too, and it’s with great enthusiasm that AutoDiag will meet their needs with new acoustic and AI-vision diagnostic technologies. The revolution is under way — you’re on the right track. Join us and share your feedback!
about.uniqueScanDesc: Our AI listens to your engine and detects 11 types of faults by sound.
about.uniqueOBDDesc: Read, analyse and clear fault codes in real time.
about.uniqueCTDesc: Prepare your MOT for 5 European countries.
about.uniqueAIDesc: Ask your questions, get answers tailored to your vehicle.
about.value_simplicity_desc: A tool anyone can use — no need to be a mechanic.
about.value_reliability_desc: Accurate diagnostics based on real data, not guesswork.
about.value_transparency_desc: Clear costs, no hidden fees, no nasty surprises.
about.value_innovation_desc: Exclusive technologies like AI audio scan and computer vision.
about.ctaTitle: Ready to give it a go?
about.ctaDesc: Join the free beta and discover the future of car diagnostics.

innovations.subtitle: Exclusive technologies that no competitor offers
innovations.desc01: Our SVM detects 11 classes of faults by sound with 91.3% accuracy. Place your phone near the bonnet — we do the rest.
innovations.desc02: 7 classes of interior noises identified automatically: rattles, squeaks, whistles and more.
innovations.desc03: Photograph a code, a part or a warning light. Instant recognition and clear explanation.
innovations.desc04: Ask your questions in plain English. Personalised answers for your vehicle, not generic copy-paste.
innovations.desc05: Accelerometer, gyroscope, GPS and your phone's microphone. No OBD2 dongle needed for a first road diagnostic.
innovations.desc06: Engine speed estimation via frequency analysis: f = (RPM x N_cyl) / (2 x 60). Surprisingly accurate.
innovations.desc07: Damping ratio zeta test via accelerometer. Assess your shock absorbers whilst driving.
innovations.desc08: Estimate real power output: P = m.a.v + resistive forces. A rolling road in your pocket.
innovations.desc09: Audio RPM and GPS speed correlation. Detects slipping before the clutch gives out completely.
innovations.desc10: Frequency analysis during braking. Identifies the characteristic vibrations of a warped disc.
innovations.desc11: Kullback-Leibler distance: we compare your engine's sound to its healthy reference signature.
innovations.desc12: Weibull model to anticipate breakdowns. Ideal for professional fleet managers.

appSimulator.alertP0420Desc: The catalytic converter is showing signs of wear. An inspection is recommended.
appSimulator.featureAudioDesc: Detect faults by the sound of your engine

audioQuiz.menuIntro: Listen to the sound samples and identify the one with a fault. Three levels to test your mechanic's ear.
audioQuiz.shareText: Virtual Stethoscope AutoDiag EU — Level {level}: {score}/3 correct answers! Test your mechanic's ear at autodiag-eu.com
audioQuiz.verdict3: Perfect! You have the absolute ear of a mechanic.
audioQuiz.verdict2: Very good! Just a little more effort for perfection.
audioQuiz.verdict1: Not bad, but your ear needs some training.
audioQuiz.verdict0: Ouch! Time to get familiar with engine sounds.
audioQuiz.desc_d1a: Normal engine — steady and stable idle
audioQuiz.desc_d2b: Normal diesel engine — regular diesel clatter
audioQuiz.desc_d2c: Normal engine — warm idle after driving
audioQuiz.desc_d3a: Normal engine — steady revs at 2,000 rpm
audioQuiz.desc_d3c: Worn belt — high-pitched squeal at start-up
audioQuiz.desc_i1c: Normal diesel engine — progressive rev increase
audioQuiz.desc_i2a: Normal turbo engine — steady boost on acceleration
audioQuiz.desc_i2b: Normal engine — return to idle after acceleration
audioQuiz.desc_i2c: Exhaust leak — rough and irregular hiss
audioQuiz.desc_i3a: Tired water pump — grinding noise under the bonnet
audioQuiz.desc_i3c: Normal V6 engine — deep and steady purr
audioQuiz.desc_e1a: Normal diesel engine — idle with typical light clatter
audioQuiz.desc_e1b: Normal diesel engine — stabilised revs at 2,500 rpm
audioQuiz.desc_e1c: Turbo whistle — high-pitched whine on acceleration
audioQuiz.desc_e2a: Knocked big-end bearing — deep rhythmic knocking
audioQuiz.desc_e2c: Normal engine — idle with air conditioning on
audioQuiz.desc_e3a: Normal engine — deceleration with engine braking
audioQuiz.desc_e3b: Normal TSI engine — idle with slight HP pump hum
audioQuiz.desc_e3c: Stretched timing chain — rapid metallic ticking when cold
audioQuiz.expl_d1b: This metallic ticking typically comes from excessive play in the hydraulic tappets or a lubrication issue. A rapid 'tick-tick-tick' noise is a classic warning sign not to be ignored.
audioQuiz.expl_d2a: This deep rumble that increases with speed is typical of a worn wheel bearing. It produces a low, continuous noise, often more noticeable when turning as the load shifts.
audioQuiz.expl_d3c: This high-pitched squeal at start-up is caused by a worn or loose auxiliary belt slipping on the pulleys. The noise is often more pronounced in cold or damp weather.
audioQuiz.expl_i1b: This irregular clatter comes from an injector that no longer atomises fuel correctly. Unlike the normal injector sound (regular and discreet), this one is louder and arrhythmic, indicating partial blockage.
audioQuiz.expl_i2c: This rough hiss accompanied by a 'pffft' sound is typical of a leak at the exhaust manifold or a failing gasket. The sound is more audible when cold and may diminish slightly when hot as the metals expand.
audioQuiz.expl_i3a: This grinding comes from the internal bearing of the water pump wearing out. If neglected, the pump can seize and cause engine overheating. Preventive replacement is recommended.
audioQuiz.expl_e1c: This high-pitched whine on acceleration is caused by excessive play in the turbocharger shaft. Unlike the slight whoosh of a healthy turbo, this metallic whistle indicates advanced bearing wear. At this stage, the turbo could fail at any moment.
audioQuiz.expl_e2a: This deep, heavy knocking, synchronised with engine speed, indicates excessive play in a connecting rod. It is one of the most serious engine noises: it often signals imminent engine failure. Switch off the engine and have the vehicle towed.
audioQuiz.expl_e3c: This rapid, metallic ticking at cold start-up, which eases slightly when warm, is typical of a stretched timing chain. The tensioner can no longer sufficiently take up the slack. This is a serious issue: a chain that jumps can destroy the engine.
